title: Classification of Common Probability Distributions
description: A flowchart that categorizes fundamental probability distributions based on data type and experimental characteristics to guide selection.
relationships: Start by determining if the random variable is Discrete or Continuous -> For Discrete: if it's a single yes/no trial, use Bernoulli; for a fixed number of independent trials, use Binomial; for the number of trials until first success, use Geometric; for the number of events in a fixed interval, use Poisson -> For Continuous: if all outcomes are equally likely, use Uniform; for natural phenomena and sums, use Normal; for modeling time until an event, use Exponential
